response.justification: A seasoned full-stack engineer with 10+ years of experience, genuine distributed-systems exposure (low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby), C/C++ at National Instruments, and strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, the resume shows no direct experience with the core of this role: experimentation/A-B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics platforms, or statistical analysis. There is also limited evidence of Principal/IC6-level cross-team architectural leadership over measurement systems. A strong generalist, but a meaningful gap against the role's specialization.
strengths: Distributed, high-throughput systems experience (millions of req/sec, low-latency)
Broad language coverage incl. C/C++, Java, C#, JS/TS plus strong CS fundamentals and a CS degree
Deep software-craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and testing tooling
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, feature-flagging, or rollout-risk experience (key requirement)
Little evidence of metrics/telemetry infrastructure or statistical/experimental analysis aptitude
Career framed at senior/staff IC level; limited proof of Principal-scale multi-workstream leadership
